opp.ais_why:
• Across 183 chars of NextStep ‚Äî essentially a single note ‚Äî the full content reads: 'After Bkk Rountable the prospect wanted a one on one via teams. Laurice and Divya provided a high level overview of Solutions. Prospect has requested for a demo in first week of march.' Zero AI mention despite Merlin Intake and ANA being in product scope.
• Product scope is eProcurement; Merlin Intake; ANA ‚Äî both AI SKUs bundled with eProcurement. The captured dialogue is BKK Roundtable-driven post-event follow-up with a 1-on-1 Teams call and a March demo planned. Laurice and Divya are named Zycus participants; no buyer-side names are captured.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is essentially empty (183 chars, one note). With Merlin Intake and ANA bundled but no captured buyer AI dialogue or named buyer-side champion, score 4 (AI Curious, boundary DOWN) reflects AI scope without AI conversation ‚Äî would re-evaluate after the planned demo.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, ROI projection, or financial impact articulated anywhere in the record. Next_Step__c notes Laurice and Divya provided a 'high level overview of Solutions' after the Bangkok Roundtable, but no business-problem discussion, cost-savings estimate, cycle-time improvement, or headcount efficiency metric was captured in any field or call transcript (zero calls on record). The 26 May disqualification on 'no budget' confirms no financial case was built to justify the investment. Without a quantified value narrative tied to the buyer's P&L or procurement KPIs, re-engagement has no commercial anchor. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery session with the buyer's procurement and finance leads to quantify the pain (current process cost, manual effort, supplier-base fragmentation, spend leakage) and model the ROI for eProcurement + Merlin Intake + ANA, then present that business case to secure budget allocation.

champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Zero OpportunityContactRole records means no Champion, Coach, or Influencer is formally mapped. Next_Step__c mentions Laurice and Divya (roles/titles unknown), who received the 'high level overview of Solutions' post-Bangkok Roundtable, but neither is marked as a champion in Salesforce, and no evidence exists of advocacy behavior (internal selling, sharing competitive intel, introducing us to the EB/DM, pushing back on competitors, driving the agenda). The 26 May 'no budget' disqualification and subsequent 36-day silence suggest neither Laurice nor Divya had the influence or access to secure funding or keep the deal alive. No X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c field is populated (SOQL Q3 null). Zero calls means no transcript evidence of champion signals (e.g. a buyer contact coaching us on internal politics, sharing the decision timeline, or advocating for Zycus in internal meetings). Without a champion, the deal has no internal advocate to fight for budget, priority, or vendor preference. The immediate move is to re-engage Laurice and Divya (if still responsive) or ask the partner to identify a senior procurement or finance stakeholder with budget influence and internal credibility, then invest in building that relationship through value delivery (tailored ROI model, executive briefing, reference-customer intro) to create advocacy.

competition.narrative: No competitors identified across any source. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are null (SOQL Q3). How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (current stack), Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, and Why_not_Zycus__c are also null. Zero Avoma calls means no transcript evidence of competitive benchmarking (e.g. 'we are also looking at Coupa/Ariba/GEP'). Zero tasks or Next_Step_History__c entries reference a competitor demo, RFP round, or competitive objection. This could mean (a) Hindsiam is sole-sourcing Zycus (unlikely given the early-stage stall and lack of urgency), (b) they are evaluating competitors but the partner/rep has not surfaced that intel (more likely, given the partner-led motion and sparse direct engagement), or (c) the deal stalled before competitive evaluation began (also plausible given the 'no budget' disqualification in Qualified stage). The lack of competitive intel is itself a risk: we are blind to whether Hindsiam is running a formal RFP, has an incumbent they are expanding, or is being courted by a rival. The immediate move is to ask the partner (or Hindsiam directly if partner is unresponsive) who else they are considering, what their current procurement stack is, and whether they have an incumbent S2P or eProcurement vendor, then position our AI/Merlin differentiation and integration strength against that landscape.

Without confirmed, owned pain, the buyer has no urgency and no internal case for budget. decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or structured use-case validation documented. The Next_Step__c field notes the prospect requested a demo of eProcurement, Merlin Intake, and ANA for 'first week of march' (early March 2026), signaling initial interest in those three product capabilities, but no evidence exists of what success looks like, what gaps in their current process these solutions must solve, or how they will score vendors. No Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, or AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields are populated (SOQL Q3 returned nulls). Zero calls means no transcript evidence of evaluation timeline, scoring rubric, pilot/POC structure, or decision gates. The deal stalled before criteria were ever formalized. The immediate move is to run a structured discovery workshop with the buyer's procurement and operations leads to define their must-have vs nice-to-have requirements, understand how AI/Merlin Intake fits their automation roadmap, and map those criteria to our strengths, creating a mutual evaluation plan that also flushes out the competition and timeline.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI appetite unmeasured. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are all null (SOQL Q2), so no AI-fit scoring exists in Salesforce. Products__c includes 'Merlin Intake', indicating AI was part of the pitched scope, but zero calls and zero AIS data mean we have no read on whether Hindsiam is AI Hungry (leadership mandate, budget earmarked for AI, procurement automation roadmap includes AI), AI Curious (interested but cautious, no budget/mandate yet), or AI Resistant (skeptical of AI, prefer traditional workflows, concerned about change management). The 'high level overview' provided by Laurice and Divya (Next_Step__c) was generic and may not have tested AI appetite. The 'no budget' disqualification could be unrelated to AI (overall procurement-software budget unavailable) or could signal that AI/Merlin was seen as a 'nice-to-have' rather than a must-have differentiator that justified securing budget. The immediate move is to score AIS in the re-engagement discovery: ask about their AI strategy, whether leadership is pushing AI adoption in procurement, whether they have pilot/POC budget for AI solutions, and how they see AI/automation fitting their procurement transformation roadmap. Then tailor the Merlin positioning: if AI Hungry, lead with Merlin as the core value prop and differentiation vs non-AI competitors; if AI Curious, frame Merlin as a future-ready add-on that grows with them; if AI Resistant, de-emphasize Merlin and lead with traditional eProcurement ROI, introducing Merlin later as a low-risk pilot.

items.detail: Disqualified on 'no budget' 26 May 2026. No Economic Buyer identified or engaged, no quantified ROI case built, no finance/procurement sponsor secured. Budget authority and FY planning cycle unknown. Risk: re-engagement without validating budget availability and EB sponsorship will stall on budget again.

No C-level, VP, or senior procurement/finance sponsor identified. Zero buyer-side contact roles. The deal has no executive air cover or internal champion to fight for budget and priority. Risk: without exec alignment, the deal remains a low-priority exploratory inquiry that will be deprioritized or killed on budget.

Partner-led APAC motion (per ground truth note) with sparse direct Zycus-buyer touchpoints. Partner has not moved the deal since 26 May 2026 disqualification (36 days dark), has not surfaced the EB or champion, and has not provided competitive intelligence or discovery depth. Risk: over-reliance on an inactive or low-capability partner leaves Zycus blind to buyer dynamics and unable to re-engage effectively. Deal team may need to engage Hindsiam directly or escalate partner management.

Demo requested for early March 2026 (~16 weeks ago) was never delivered (no tasks, no calls, no follow-up evidence). Failure to deliver a core buyer ask signals low urgency on Zycus/partner side or that the prospect lost interest. Risk: re-engaging without acknowledging the missed demo and resetting expectations will erode credibility.

Zero competitive intelligence. Unknown whether Hindsiam is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a formal RFP with multiple vendors, or has an incumbent S2P/eProcurement vendor they are expanding or replacing. Blind to competitive threats and cannot tailor differentiation or counter objections. Risk: a competitor may be winning by default while we remain dormant.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Laurice and Divya are the only named buyer contacts (Next_Step__c), but neither is designated as Champion in Salesforce (zero OpportunityContactRole records), and no evidence exists of advocacy behavior (internal selling, EB/DM introductions, competitive coaching, agenda-driving). The 26 May 'no budget' disqualification and 36-day silence indicate neither had the influence to secure funding or keep the deal alive. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is null. Zero calls means no transcript evidence of champion signals. Without a champion, the deal has no internal advocate to fight for budget, priority, or vendor preference, and re-engagement has no friendly entry point. High risk of ghosting or permanent stall without champion development.

items.commitment: Deliver demo of eProcurement, Merlin Intake, and ANA solutions requested by Hindsiam

items.action: Executive connect led by the deal owner's manager to the relevant stakeholder or VP Procurement to validate FY27 budget availability, understand procurement-transformation priority, and secure executive sponsorship. Frame as exploring whether Zycus S2P + AI/Merlin aligns with their strategic roadmap, not as chasing the stalled demo. Goal: map the Economic Buyer, confirm budget timeline, and assess whether this is a real FY27 opportunity or should be retired.
items.trigger: Disqualified on 'no budget' 26 May 2026; 36 days dark; no EB or exec sponsor identified
items.expected_effect: Validates whether opportunity is recoverable (budget exists, just not allocated yet) or should be closed-lost. If recoverable, secures EB sponsorship and pathway to budget allocation, moving deal from Qualified to the relevant stakeholder unrecoverable, frees up deal-team capacity.

items.action: Partner escalation: deal owner engages partner leadership to understand why the deal has been dark for 36 days post-disqualification, demand a written account of partner's relationship with Hindsiam (who they know, what access they have, what discovery they have done), and set a 7-day deadline for partner to either (a) secure a buyer meeting with EB/procurement lead, or (b) hand back the account to Zycus for direct engagement. If partner cannot move the deal, Zycus takes over buyer engagement directly.
items.trigger: Partner-led APAC motion has produced zero activity since 26 May 2026 disqualification; no EB surfaced, no competitive intel, no discovery depth
items.expected_effect: Clarifies partner capability and commitment. Either re-activates partner to drive EB meeting and discovery (recovering the deal through the partner channel), or transfers control to Zycus for direct engagement, preventing further drift.

items.action: Conduct discovery workshop (virtual, 60-90 min) with Hindsiam procurement and finance leads (ideally including EB/VP Procurement) to map current-state procurement process, quantify pain (manual PO/invoice cost, cycle time, supplier-base fragmentation, spend visibility gaps), understand ERP/system landscape, assess AI appetite and procurement-transformation roadmap, and surface competitive landscape (incumbent vendors, other evaluations in flight). Deliverable: tailored ROI model showing cost savings and efficiency gains from eProcurement + Merlin Intake + ANA, plus a mutual evaluation plan with timeline and success criteria. This replaces the missed March 2026 generic demo with a business-outcome-focused session that creates urgency and justifies budget.
items.trigger: No quantified business case, no pain owner identified, no discovery depth; demo requested March 2026 was never delivered
items.expected_effect: Builds quantified business case that can justify budget allocation in FY27 planning. Maps pain owner and decision process. Creates champion development opportunity (whoever advocates internally for the workshop becomes a potential champion). Surfaces competitive landscape. Moves deal from exploratory/stalled to structured evaluation with mutual commitments.

items.action: Map Hindsiam org chart and identify Economic Buyer and Decision Maker: use LinkedIn, partner intel, or direct outreach to identify CFO, VP Procurement, Head of Supply Chain, and their reporting structure. Confirm who controls FY27 CapEx/OpEx budget for procurement software, who owns procurement-transformation initiatives, and who must sign contracts over $150k. Update OpportunityContactRole records with EB and DM designations and plan targeted outreach (exec briefing, ROI model, reference-customer intro) to secure their sponsorship.
items.trigger: Zero OpportunityContactRole records; no EB, DM, or exec sponsor identified; disqualified on 'no budget' indicates the people engaged (Laurice, Divya) lacked authority
items.expected_effect: Identifies the person who can allocate budget and approve the deal, enabling targeted EB engagement. Prevents repeat of 'no budget' stall by ensuring future conversations include the authority to commit funds. Provides pathway to executive sponsorship and champion development.

items.action: Surface competitive landscape: in the discovery workshop or EB outreach, ask Hindsiam directly: (1) who else are you evaluating for S2P/eProcurement, (2) what is your current procurement stack (incumbent vendors, ERP system), (3) are you running a formal RFP or sole-sourcing, (4) what is your timeline and evaluation criteria. Document findings in Competitors__c and competitive_position. Tailor Zycus differentiation (AI/Merlin, integration strength, customer success in beverage/CPG vertical) against the named competitors. If sole-sourced, understand why and leverage that urgency; if competitive, build the wedge and activation plan to win.
items.trigger: Zero competitive intelligence (Competitors__c null, no intel from calls/tasks); blind to whether Hindsiam is sole-sourcing, running RFP, or has incumbent
items.expected_effect: Removes competitive blind spot, enabling tailored positioning and objection-handling. If a strong competitor is present, allows Zycus to build differentiation narrative and counter-positioning. If sole-sourced, accelerates deal by leaning into the existing preference and urgency.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 10 Feb 2026, 141 days in Qualified stage with no progression signals. Close date 2027-05-31 is 334 days out. Zero forward slip recorded because the deal has never advanced beyond Qualified. Time-in-stage is 4.7 months with no buyer engagement on record (LastActivityDate unknown, no Avoma calls discovered). The next-step field logs a 26 May 2026 disqualification on 'no budget' and a March 2026 demo request that was never fulfilled. The deal has been in qualified-but-inactive state for the majority of its life, and the last known touch (disqualification) is 36 days old as of today (01 Jul 2026). Required pace: this stage should drive discovery, MEDDPICC clarity, and champion-building within 30-45 days; at 141 days with zero engagement the deal is severely behind required pace and unrecoverable without immediate re-engagement and budget validation.
evidence: Next_Step__c logs '0% Confidence' and '26th May- qualified out- no budget', marking an explicit disqualification 36 days ago (26 May 2026).

north_star_verdict.headline: Disqualified on budget 26 May 2026, now 36 days dark with zero buyer engagement, no EB mapped, no champion secured, and a missed demo commitment from March 2026. Partner-led motion has stalled. Re-engagement requires validating budget availability and securing an executive sponsor before any further technical discussion.

flags: No Economic Buyer mapped or engaged. The 26 May 2026 disqualification on 'no budget' signals budget authority was never reached. In a $150k S2P deal, the CFO, CPO, or VP Finance/Procurement must approve the investment. Re-engagement must map and engage the EB directly (with the partner's help or around them if the partner cannot access) to validate budget availability, FY planning cycle, and appetite for procurement transformation before proposing commercials again. Without EB buy-in, this deal will stall on budget a second time.

Zero buyer-side contact roles and no champion. Krishnakumar Gopalasamy (Director of Procurement) is an account-level contact but not linked to this opportunity, and no evidence exists of his involvement or advocacy in this S2P deal. A $150k enterprise procurement platform requires a multi-threaded stakeholder map (procurement lead, finance approver, IT/integration owner, end-user representatives) and a champion who will fight internally for budget and priority. The partner-led motion has not surfaced or connected Zycus to these stakeholders. The deal team must add the missing buyer-side contacts to the opportunity as contact roles, confirm their involvement in this S2P scope, and develop a champion who has access to the EB and will advocate for Zycus over any alternative or 'do nothing'. This is not CRM hygiene; it is the foundation for re-engagement and win strategy.

Competitive intelligence is absent. No competitor, incumbent, or alternative solution is documented, so Zycus cannot position its differentiation (Merlin AI for intake/negotiation, integration depth, ROI speed) or counter a rival's pitch. The disqualification on budget could mean Hindsiam chose status quo, lost to a lower-cost competitor, or genuinely had no FY budget allocated. Re-engagement discovery must surface the competitive reality: is Hindsiam evaluating other S2P vendors now (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, regional platforms), locked into a legacy ERP procurement module, or simply not prioritizing procurement investment in 2026? If a competitor exists, the deal team must understand their positioning and pricing, then build a wedge around Merlin AI agentic capabilities (Intake to PO automation, negotiation intelligence, supplier analytics) that justifies the $150k premium and ties to a quantified ROI case Hindsiam's finance team will approve.
DARK: 31 days since last buyer touch (26 May disqualification), no rep or partner outreach, no re-engagement plan (source: engagement pulse, SF Next_Step__c)

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is severely off best-practice: disqualified on 'no budget' 26 May 2026, now 36 days dark with zero buyer engagement signals. Single-threaded (only Laurice and Divya mentioned, roles unknown, no contact roles on record). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion mapped. No quantified business case. Demo requested March 2026 was never delivered. Partner-led motion has stalled with no partner activity since disqualification. Close date 2027-05-31 (334 days out) is aspirational with no path to progression. Stage should be Pipeline (exploratory, no budget, no EB) not Qualified. Forecast Omitted is correct. Immediate action: re-engage to validate budget timeline and EB access, or retire the opportunity.

competitive_position.summary: No competitors identified. Zero competitive intelligence across Salesforce fields (Competitors__c null), Next_Step__c, tasks, or Avoma calls (none discovered). Unknown whether Hindsiam is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a formal multi-vendor RFP, or has an incumbent S2P vendor they are expanding. This blind spot is a critical risk: we cannot position differentiation, tailor messaging, or counter competitive objections without knowing the landscape. The partner-led APAC motion has not surfaced competitive intel. Immediate action required: ask the partner or Hindsiam directly who else they are evaluating, what their current procurement stack is, and whether they have an incumbent vendor, then position Zycus AI/Merlin and integration strengths against that context.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning unknown and likely weak. Products__c includes 'Merlin Intake' (our AI-driven intake automation solution), signaling that AI was part of the initial scope pitched at the Bangkok Roundtable and in the high-level overview provided by Laurice and Divya. However, no AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c fields are populated (SOQL Q2 nulls), so AI excitement/appetite is unmeasured. Zero calls means no transcript evidence of how Merlin/AI resonated with the buyer, whether they have an AI roadmap or procurement-automation use cases that Merlin solves, or whether AI differentiation was a factor in their demo request. The 'no budget' disqualification and lack of follow-through on the demo suggest AI positioning (if discussed at all) did not create enough urgency or differentiation to overcome budget barriers. Risk: if Hindsiam is AI-curious or has AI mandates from leadership, and we failed to land the Merlin narrative as a transformational differentiator (vs commodity eProcurement), we may have under-positioned and commoditized ourselves. Conversely, if they are AI-resistant or not ready for Merlin's sophistication, we may have over-positioned and scared them off. Without AIS scoring or call evidence, we are blind. The immediate move is to assess their AI maturity and appetite in the re-engagement discovery call (do they have AI initiatives, what is leadership's stance on AI in procurement, are they measuring AI ROI) and tailor the Merlin narrative accordingly—either as a core differentiator (if AI Hungry) or as a future-ready optional add-on (if AI Curious/Resistant).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Insufficient evidence to assess fit. Hindsiam requested a demo of eProcurement, Merlin Intake, and ANA (Products__c), signaling interest in procurement automation and AI-driven intake, which aligns with Zycus's core S2P suite and Merlin portfolio. However, no discovery depth exists: no documented business requirements, no evaluation criteria, no pain quantification, no use-case validation. The 'high level overview' provided by Laurice and Divya (Next_Step__c) was generic, not tailored to Hindsiam's specific procurement challenges, and the deal stalled on 'no budget' before fit could be tested through a demo, workshop, or pilot. Zero calls means no transcript evidence of buyer reactions to our positioning, feature gaps, or integration concerns. Fit remains unproven. The risk is that we assume alignment based on product interest alone, but without understanding their operational context (ERP system, supplier base size, current procurement maturity, AI readiness), we may discover late-stage misalignment (e.g. integration complexity, change-management resistance, or feature gaps). The immediate move is to conduct a discovery session to map their current-state procurement process, system landscape, and automation priorities, then validate that eProcurement + Merlin Intake + ANA genuinely solve their highest-pain use cases before investing in a full demo or workshop.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 183 chars of NextStep ‚Äî essentially a single note ‚Äî the full content reads: 'After Bkk Rountable the prospect wanted a one on one via teams. Laurice and Divya provided a high level overview of Solutions. Prospect has requested for a demo in first week of march.' Zero AI mention despite Merlin Intake and ANA being in product scope.
• Product scope is eProcurement; Merlin Intake; ANA ‚Äî both AI SKUs bundled with eProcurement. The captured dialogue is BKK Roundtable-driven post-event follow-up with a 1-on-1 Teams call and a March demo planned. Laurice and Divya are named Zycus participants; no buyer-side names are captured.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is essentially empty (183 chars, one note). With Merlin Intake and ANA bundled but no captured buyer AI dialogue or named buyer-side champion, score 4 (AI Curious, boundary DOWN) reflects AI scope without AI conversation ‚Äî would re-evaluate after the planned demo.

No Economic Buyer mapped or engaged. The 26 May 2026 disqualification on 'no budget' signals budget authority was never reached. In a $150k S2P deal, the CFO, CPO, or VP Finance/Procurement must approve the investment. Re-engagement must map and engage the EB directly (with the partner's help or around them if the partner cannot access) to validate budget availability, FY planning cycle, and appetite for procurement transformation before proposing commercials again. Without EB buy-in, this deal will stall on budget a second time.

Zero buyer-side contact roles and no champion. Krishnakumar Gopalasamy (Director of Procurement) is an account-level contact but not linked to this opportunity, and no evidence exists of his involvement or advocacy in this S2P deal. A $150k enterprise procurement platform requires a multi-threaded stakeholder map (procurement lead, finance approver, IT/integration owner, end-user representatives) and a champion who will fight internally for budget and priority. The partner-led motion has not surfaced or connected Zycus to these stakeholders. The deal team must add the missing buyer-side contacts to the opportunity as contact roles, confirm their involvement in this S2P scope, and develop a champion who has access to the EB and will advocate for Zycus over any alternative or 'do nothing'. This is not CRM hygiene; it is the foundation for re-engagement and win strategy.

Competitive intelligence is absent. No competitor, incumbent, or alternative solution is documented, so Zycus cannot position its differentiation (Merlin AI for intake/negotiation, integration depth, ROI speed) or counter a rival's pitch. The disqualification on budget could mean Hindsiam chose status quo, lost to a lower-cost competitor, or genuinely had no FY budget allocated. Re-engagement discovery must surface the competitive reality: is Hindsiam evaluating other S2P vendors now (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, regional platforms), locked into a legacy ERP procurement module, or simply not prioritizing procurement investment in 2026? If a competitor exists, the deal team must understand their positioning and pricing, then build a wedge around Merlin AI agentic capabilities (Intake to PO automation, negotiation intelligence, supplier analytics) that justifies the $150k premium and ties to a quantified ROI case Hindsiam's finance team will approve.

Re-engagement discovery must surface the competitive reality: is Hindsiam evaluating other S2P vendors now (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, regional platforms), locked into a legacy ERP procurement module, or simply not prioritizing procurement investment in 2026? If a competitor exists, the deal team must understand their positioning and pricing, then build a wedge around Merlin AI agentic capabilities (Intake to PO automation, negotiation intelligence, supplier analytics) that justifies the $150k premium and ties to a quantified ROI case Hindsiam's finance team will approve.

gaps: Zero Avoma calls discovered across both opp-id (006P700000TWaHP 15-char: 006P700000TWaHP) and account-id (Account.Id from SOQL Q1) discovery, using dutchmill.co.th domain matching. Either (a) no buyer calls have been recorded/transcribed in Avoma for this deal, or (b) attendee email domains do not match dutchmill.co.th and manual attendee-name matching is required. This is a genuine knowledge gap (no call content to synthesize MEDDPICC depth from).

Zero OpportunityContactRole records exist in Salesforce (SOQL query returned empty set). Stakeholder map, EB/DM/Champion identification, and multi-threading analysis all rely on manually inferring from Next_Step__c mentions (Laurice, Divya) with no roles, titles, emails, or formal designation. This is a hygiene gap (contacts engaged but not logged in SF) that creates a downstream knowledge gap (cannot assess stakeholder influence, sentiment, or champion potential without roles/titles/context).

Zero Salesforce Task or Event records on this opportunity in the trailing 90 days (SOQL query returned empty set). No evidence of follow-up calls, emails, meetings, or commitments logged. This limits visibility into what was actually delivered (e.g. whether the March 2026 demo request was fulfilled) and what commitments were made. This is a hygiene gap (activity may have occurred but was not logged) that creates knowledge gaps around deliverables and engagement cadence.

AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are all null (SOQL Q2). No AI-fit scoring or AI-appetite assessment exists in Salesforce. Products__c includes Merlin Intake (AI product), but no read on whether buyer is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant. This is a hygiene gap (AIS scoring process not completed) that creates a knowledge gap (cannot tailor AI/Merlin positioning without knowing buyer's AI maturity and appetite).

LastActivityDate is unknown/null per ground truth. Salesforce has no logged buyer engagement date, corroborating the DARK engagement state and zero-call/zero-task findings. This is a hygiene gap (activity logging incomplete) that prevents accurate engagement-recency and cooling-risk assessment.

Next_Step_History__c field was included in SOQL Q2 but returned null or was not populated in the org. This limits visibility into the deal's movement timeline and rep narrative over time. Relied on Next_Step__c current value and LastModifiedDate instead. This is a hygiene gap (field not used or not available in this org).

All MEDDPICC and competition synthesis-source fields (SOQL Q3: Competitors__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Compelling_Event__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Business_Requirements__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, and 15+ other Q3 fields) returned null. These fields are the primary Salesforce source for MEDDPICC and competitive synthesis. Their absence, combined with zero calls and zero contact roles, means MEDDPICC coverage is a genuine knowledge gap across all elements (Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, Competition). This is NOT a hygiene gap—it is a true knowledge gap because the information does not exist anywhere in the available evidence (not in fields, not in calls, not in tasks, not in contact roles).
